mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean during rate decline periods across a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment surge, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (accuracy < 55%) over a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and near-term corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth fails to yield positive 5-day returns in > 60% of observations during early-cycle phases, or predictive alpha decays to zero within 10 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation combined with persistently low nominal policy rates compresses real yields and signals impending monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS yields positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term shows no marginal predictive power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly nonfarm payroll employment growth supports aggregate consumption and corporate revenue expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerating below 1.5 percent quarter-over-quarter signals weakening aggregate demand and reduced near-term earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 70% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below the 1.5 percent threshold, invalidating the slowdown signal.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in industrial production and decrease in the unemployment rate confirms a strengthening economic cycle with robust output and labor absorption, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergent movement of INDPRO and UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns (accuracy < 50%) during mid-cycle phases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.
